note: Search API is a framework module; the load-bearing facts are its two config entities (server/index), its plugin type ids + namespaces, the search_api_db backend id, drush command names, and the admin permission. Most cases are `recipe` (response-graded); one `execution` case builds a live database-backed index and is graded against site state.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), stand up a working Search API setup backed by the database. Create a Search API server named `eval_db_server` using the Database backend, and an index named `eval_index` that indexes Article nodes (the node datasource for the article bundle) with at least the node title added as an indexed field. Apply it live and index the content; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: A search_api_server `eval_db_server` (backend search_api_db) and an enabled search_api_index `eval_index` exist, the index writes to that server, uses the entity:node datasource, and has the node title added as an indexed field (property path title, type text/string). Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: In Search API on Drupal 11, which two config entity types tie a search setup together, and what does each one hold? Name the entity type machine ids.
evals.expected_output: The Server config entity (search_api_server) chooses a backend plugin + connection settings; the Index config entity (search_api_index) chooses datasource(s), the server to write to, a tracker, fields, and the processor pipeline.

evals.prompt: I want to write a custom Search API backend for a new search engine. What is the plugin type id, the base class I extend, and which directory / namespace does the plugin class live in?
evals.expected_output: Plugin type id search_api_backend, extend BackendPluginBase (attribute #[SearchApiBackend]), placed in Plugin/search_api/backend/ of the module (namespace Drupal\<module>\Plugin\search_api\backend).

evals.prompt: Besides backends, name the Search API plugin types used to (a) provide the source of indexable items, (b) transform data at index/search time, and (c) queue which items still need indexing. Give the plugin type ids.

I want a search server that stores its index in the site's database using the submodule that ships with Search API, without installing Solr. Which submodule do I enable and what is its backend plugin id that I select on the server?
evals.expected_output: Enable the search_api_db submodule and choose the search_api_db backend on the Server.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, give the drush commands to (1) index all tracked items for an index and (2) delete/wipe all indexed data for an index.
evals.expected_output: drush search-api:index [INDEX] to index tracked items, and drush search-api:clear [INDEX] to delete all indexed data for the index.

evals.prompt: Which single permission gates the entire Search API admin UI (creating/configuring servers, indexes, fields, processors, trackers)? Give its machine name.
evals.expected_output: The 'administer search_api' permission (defined in search_api.permissions.yml) gates the whole admin UI at /admin/config/search/search-api.

evals.prompt: In PHP on a Drupal 11 site, how do I build and run a Search API keyword search against an index config entity named 'default_index' and get the result items? Show the key calls.
evals.expected_output: Load the search_api_index entity, call $index->query() to get a QueryInterface, chain keys()/addCondition()/sort()/range(), then $query->execute() returns a ResultSetInterface whose getResultItems() you iterate.
